steps.title: Put the equation in standard form
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side:
$$kx^2-18x=20x^2-9$$
$$(k-20)x^2-18x+9=0$$

steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition
steps.content:
For two distinct real solutions, the discriminant must be positive:
$$b^2-4ac>0$$
Here $a=k-20$, $b=-18$, and $c=9$, so
$$(-18)^2-4(k-20)(9)>0$$

steps.title: Find the greatest integer
steps.content: Solve the inequality: $$324-36(k-20)>0$$ $$1{,}044-36k>0$$ $$k<29$$ The greatest integer less than $29$ is $28$. The answer is 28.
